sourceMovie.title: Young Guns
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Young Guns (1988) as a western theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The western and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Magnificent Seven
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Young Guns clicked for you, The Magnificent Seven (1960) is a strong next stop: both trade in western and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Young Guns clicked for you, The Cowboys (1972) is a strong next stop: both trade in western and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Young Guns?
faq.answer: Try The Magnificent Seven, The Cowboys, Conan the Barbarian—each shares DNA with Young Guns while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Young Guns?
faq.answer: Young Guns (1988) is best described as Western, Action, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Young Guns part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Young Guns Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
